attachment.content:
- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

+class S3AuditLogReader:
+    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
+
+    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
+    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
+    """
+
+    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
+        self.bucket: str = bucket
+        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
+        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
+
+    def _key_for(self, id: str) -> str:
+        if self.prefix:
+            return f"{self.prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
+        return f"{id}.jsonl.gz"
+
+    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
+        key = self._key_for(id)
+        try:
+            response = self.s3.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)
+        except ClientError as e:
+            error_code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
+            if error_code in ("NoSuchKey", "404", "NoSuchBucket"):
+                return []
+            raise
+
+        body_bytes: bytes = response["Body"].read()
+        decompressed = gzip.decompress(body_bytes).decode("utf-8")
+        entries: list[AuditEntry] = []
+        for line in decompressed.splitlines():
+            stripped = line.strip()
+            if not stripped:
+                continue
+            entries.append(AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(stripped)))  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
+        return entries

+def _make_stub_s3(body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None) -> Any:
+    """Return a minimal stub for boto3 S3 client."""
+    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
+
+    stub = MagicMock()
+    if error_code:
+        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(
+            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
+        )
+    elif body is not None:
+        response_body = MagicMock()
+        response_body.read.return_value = body
+        stub.get_object.return_value = {"Body": response_body}
+    return stub
+

+                "timestamp": "2026-05-07T15:00:00+00:00",
+                "message": "second",
+            },
+        ]
+    )
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="my-bucket", prefix="audit", s3_client=s3)
+
+    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")
+
+    assert len(results) == 2
+    assert results[0].message == "first"
+    assert results[1].message == "second"
+    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(

246	+class S3AuditLogReader:
247	+    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
248	+
249	+    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
250	+    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
251	+    """
252	+
253	+    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
254	+        self.bucket: str = bucket
255	+        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
256	+        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
257	+
258	+    def _key_for(self, id: str) -> str:
259	+        if self.prefix:
260	+            return f"{self.prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
261	+        return f"{id}.jsonl.gz"
262	+
263	+    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
264	+        key = self._key_for(id)
265	+        try:
266	+            response = self.s3.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)
267	+        except ClientError as e:
268	+            error_code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
269	+            if error_code in ("NoSuchKey", "404", "NoSuchBucket"):
270	+                return []
271	+            raise
272	+
273	+        body_bytes: bytes = response["Body"].read()
274	+        decompressed = gzip.decompress(body_bytes).decode("utf-8")
275	+        entries: list[AuditEntry] = []
276	+        for line in decompressed.splitlines():
277	+            stripped = line.strip()
278	+            if not stripped:
279	+                continue
280	+            entries.append(AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(stripped)))  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
281	+        return entries
282	+

284	+class FallbackAuditLogReader:
285	+    """Reads audit logs from a primary source, falling back to S3 when empty."""
286	+
287	+    def __init__(self, primary: AuditLogger, fallback: S3AuditLogReader) -> None:
288	+        self.primary: AuditLogger = primary
289	+        self.fallback: S3AuditLogReader = fallback
290	+
291	+    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
292	+        primary_results = self.primary.get_logs(id)
293	+        if primary_results:
294	+            return primary_results
295	+        logger.info(
296	+            "No audit logs found in primary store for id %s, falling back to S3", id
297	+        )
298	+        return self.fallback.get_logs(id)
299	+

702	+        "message": "hello",
703	+    }

706	+# --- S3AuditLogReader ---
707	+
708	+
709	+def _make_stub_s3(body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None) -> Any:
710	+    """Return a minimal stub for boto3 S3 client."""
711	+    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
712	+
713	+    stub = MagicMock()
714	+    if error_code:
715	+        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(
716	+            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
717	+        )
718	+    elif body is not None:
719	+        response_body = MagicMock()
720	+        response_body.read.return_value = body
721	+        stub.get_object.return_value = {"Body": response_body}
722	+    return stub
723	+
724	+
725	+def test_s3_reader_returns_entries_from_gzipped_jsonl():
726	+    ts = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
727	+    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
728	+        [
729	+            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts, "message": "first"},
730	+            {
731	+                "id": "req-1",
732	+                "timestamp": "2026-05-07T15:00:00+00:00",
733	+                "message": "second",
734	+            },
735	+        ]
736	+    )
737	+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
738	+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="my-bucket", prefix="audit", s3_client=s3)
739	+
740	+    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")
741	+
742	+    assert len(results) == 2
743	+    assert results[0].message == "first"
744	+    assert results[1].message == "second"
745	+    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(
746	+        Bucket="my-bucket", Key="audit/req-1.jsonl.gz"
747	+    )
748	+
749	+
750	+def test_s3_reader_uses_prefix_in_key():
751	+    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
752	+        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
753	+    )
754	+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
755	+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="some/prefix", s3_client=s3)
756	+
757	+    reader.get_logs("x")
758	+
759	+    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz")
760	+
761	+
762	+def test_s3_reader_strips_trailing_slash_from_prefix():
763	+    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
764	+        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
765	+    )
766	+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
767	+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="some/prefix/", s3_client=s3)
768	+
769	+    reader.get_logs("x")
770	+
771	+    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz")
772	+
773	+
774	+def test_s3_reader_empty_prefix_omits_slash():

777	+    )
778	+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)

781	+    reader.get_logs("x")

787	+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="NoSuchKey")

789	+

801	+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="AccessDenied")
802	+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)
803	+
804	+    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

diff --git a/.gitignore b/.gitignore
index 7b92680..28ff6ff 100644
--- a/.gitignore
+++ b/.gitignore
@@ -320,3 +320,6 @@ report.xml
 .aws-sam-requirements.txt
 # Ignore build folder
 .aws-sam/
+
+# Local SAM debug fixtures (may contain real IDs or env-specific values)
+tests/fixtures/
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index fc9b124..b750b71 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 # This is a Makefile for building packages to deploy to AWS with AWS SAM
 .DEFAULT_GOAL := help
 
-.PHONY: help clean build-CreateFunction build-ProcessFunction
+.PHONY: help clean build-CreateFunction build-ProcessFunction build-ReporterFunction
 
 help: ## Show help
 	@echo "Usage: make [target]"
@@ -19,5 +19,11 @@ build-ProcessFunction:
 	cp -r src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/"
 	rm -f .aws-sam-requirements.txt
 
+build-ReporterFunction:
+	uv export --no-dev --format requirements-txt -o .aws-sam-requirements.txt
+	uv pip install --python 3.13 --target "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/" -r .aws-sam-requirements.txt
+	cp -r src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/"
+	rm -f .aws-sam-requirements.txt
+
 clean:
 	rm -f .aws-sam-requirements.txt

+class S3AuditLogReader:
+    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
+
+    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
+    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
+    """
+
+    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
+        self.bucket: str = bucket
+        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
+        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
+
+    def _key_for(self, id: str) -> str:
+        if self.prefix:
+            return f"{self.prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
+        return f"{id}.jsonl.gz"
+
+    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
+        key = self._key_for(id)
+        try:
+            response = self.s3.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)
+        except ClientError as e:
+            error_code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
+            if error_code in ("NoSuchKey", "404", "NoSuchBucket"):
+                return []
+            raise
+
+        body_bytes: bytes = response["Body"].read()
+        decompressed = gzip.decompress(body_bytes).decode("utf-8")
+        entries: list[AuditEntry] = []
+        for line in decompressed.splitlines():
+            stripped = line.strip()
+            if not stripped:
+                continue
+            entries.append(AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(stripped)))  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
+        return entries
+
+
+class FallbackAuditLogReader:
+    """Reads audit logs from a primary source, falling back to S3 when empty."""
+
+    def __init__(self, primary: AuditLogger, fallback: S3AuditLogReader) -> None:
+        self.primary: AuditLogger = primary
+        self.fallback: S3AuditLogReader = fallback
+
+    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
+        primary_results = self.primary.get_logs(id)
+        if primary_results:
+            return primary_results
+        logger.info(
+            "No audit logs found in primary store for id %s, falling back to S3", id
+        )
+        return self.fallback.get_logs(id)
+

+def get_s3_audit_log_reader(bucket: str, prefix: str) -> S3AuditLogReader:
+    s3: S3Client = boto3.client("s3")
+    return S3AuditLogReader(bucket=bucket, prefix=prefix, s3_client=s3)

+def get_audit_log_reader() -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
+    """Get an audit log reader configured from environment variables.
+
+    Uses the DynamoDB-backed audit logger as the primary source and an S3
+    reader as the fallback. Both must be configured via environment.
+    """
+    primary: AuditLogger = get_audit_logger()
+
+    bucket: str | None = os.getenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET)
+    if bucket is None or bucket == "":
+        raise ValueError(f"{AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET} was not set")
+    prefix: str = os.getenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "")
+
+    fallback = get_s3_audit_log_reader(bucket=bucket, prefix=prefix)
+    return FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback)

+            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts, "message": "first"},
+            {
+                "id": "req-1",
+                "timestamp": "2026-05-07T15:00:00+00:00",
+                "message": "second",
+            },
+        ]
+    )
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="my-bucket", prefix="audit", s3_client=s3)
+
+    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")
+
+    assert len(results) == 2
+    assert results[0].message == "first"
+    assert results[1].message == "second"
+    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(
+        Bucket="my-bucket", Key="audit/req-1.jsonl.gz"
+    )
+
+
+def test_s3_reader_uses_prefix_in_key():
+    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
+        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
+    )
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="some/prefix", s3_client=s3)
+
+    reader.get_logs("x")
+
+    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz")
+
+
+def test_s3_reader_strips_trailing_slash_from_prefix():
+    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
+        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
+    )
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="some/prefix/", s3_client=s3)
+
+    reader.get_logs("x")
+
+    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz")
+
+
+def test_s3_reader_empty_prefix_omits_slash():
+    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
+        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
+    )
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="", s3_client=s3)
+
+    reader.get_logs("x")
+
+    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="bucket", Key="x.jsonl.gz")
+
+
+def test_s3_reader_returns_empty_on_no_such_key():
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="NoSuchKey")
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)
+
+    assert reader.get_logs("missing-id") == []
+
+
+def test_s3_reader_returns_empty_on_no_such_bucket():
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="NoSuchBucket")
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)
+
+    assert reader.get_logs("any-id") == []
+
+
+def test_s3_reader_propagates_other_client_errors():
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="AccessDenied")
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)
+
+    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
+
+    with pytest.raises(ClientError):
+        reader.get_logs("req-1")
+
+
+def test_s3_reader_raises_on_malformed_json():
+    bad_bytes = gzip.compress(
+        b'{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00"\n{bad line'
+    )
+    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=bad_bytes)
+    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)
+
+    with pytest.raises(json.JSONDecodeError):
+        reader.get_logs("x")
+
+
+# --- FallbackAuditLogReader ---
+
+
+def test_fallback_reader_returns_primary_when_non_empty():
+    ts = datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
+    primary = InMemoryAuditLogger()
+    primary.log("req-1", "primary entry", ts)
+
+    fallback_s3 = MagicMock(spec=S3AuditLogReader)
+
+    reader = FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback_s3)
+    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")
+
+    assert len(results) == 1
+    assert results[0].message == "primary entry"
+    fallback_s3.get_logs.assert_not_called()
+
+
+def test_fallback_reader_falls_back_to_s3_when_primary_empty():
+    primary = InMemoryAuditLogger()
+    ts = datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
+    expected = [AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=ts, message="s3 entry")]
+
+    fallback_s3 = MagicMock(spec=S3AuditLogReader)
+    fallback_s3.get_logs.return_value = expected
+
+    reader = FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback_s3)
+    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")
+
+    assert results == expected
+    fallback_s3.get_logs.assert_called_once_with("req-1")
+
+
+def test_fallback_reader_returns_empty_when_both_empty():
+    primary = InMemoryAuditLogger()
+    fallback_s3 = MagicMock(spec=S3AuditLogReader)
+    fallback_s3.get_logs.return_value = []
+
+    reader = FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback_s3)
+    assert reader.get_logs("req-1") == []
+
+
+# --- Factory functions ---
+
+
 def test_get_audit_logger_returns_noop_logger_without_env(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
     monkeypatch.delenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, raising=False)
 
@@ -192,3 +536,49 @@ def test_get_dynamodb_audit_logger_builds_boto3_client(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch)
     assert isinstance(logger, DynamoDBAuditLogger)
     assert logger.table_name == "audit-table"
     assert logger.dynamodb is client
+
+
+def test_get_s3_audit_log_reader_builds_boto3_client(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
+    s3_stub = MagicMock()
+    calls: list[str] = []
+
+    def fake_boto3_client(service_name: str) -> Any:
+        calls.append(service_name)
+        return s3_stub
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        "dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.boto3.client", fake_boto3_client
+    )
+
+    reader = get_s3_audit_log_reader("my-bucket", "my-prefix")
+
+    assert isinstance(reader, S3AuditLogReader)
+    assert reader.bucket == "my-bucket"
+    assert reader.prefix == "my-prefix"
+    assert "s3" in calls
+
+
+def test_get_audit_log_reader_raises_without_s3_bucket(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
+    monkeypatch.delenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, raising=False)
+    monkeypatch.setenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "pfx")
+
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET):
+        get_audit_log_reader()
+
+
+def test_get_audit_log_reader_uses_empty_prefix_by_default(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
+    monkeypatch.setenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "my-bucket")
+    monkeypatch.delenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, raising=False)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        "dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.boto3.client",
+        lambda _: MagicMock(),
+    )
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        "dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.get_audit_logger",
+        lambda: NoOpAuditLogger(),
+    )
+
+    reader = get_audit_log_reader()
+
+    assert isinstance(reader, FallbackAuditLogReader)
+    assert reader.fallback.prefix == ""

import gzip
import json
from collections.abc import Generator
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Literal

import boto3
import pytest
from moto import mock_aws

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import S3AuditLogReader

TEST_BUCKET: Literal["test-audit-bucket"] = "test-audit-bucket"
TEST_PREFIX: Literal["audit-logs"] = "audit-logs"


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


@pytest.fixture
def s3_bucket() -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    with mock_aws():
        s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
        s3.create_bucket(
            Bucket=TEST_BUCKET,
            CreateBucketConfiguration={"LocationConstraint": "eu-west-1"},
        )
        yield


def build_reader() -> S3AuditLogReader:
    s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
    return S3AuditLogReader(bucket=TEST_BUCKET, prefix=TEST_PREFIX, s3_client=s3)


def upload(id: str, entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> None:
    s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
    key = f"{TEST_PREFIX}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
    s3.put_object(Bucket=TEST_BUCKET, Key=key, Body=_make_gzipped_jsonl(entries))


def test_get_logs_returns_entries_in_file_order(s3_bucket: None):
    ts1 = "2026-05-07T10:00:00+00:00"
    ts2 = "2026-05-07T11:00:00+00:00"
    ts3 = "2026-05-07T12:00:00+00:00"
    upload(
        "req-1",
        [
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts1, "message": "first"},
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts2, "message": "second"},
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts3, "message": "third"},
        ],
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 3
    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["first", "second", "third"]


def test_get_logs_parses_timestamps_correctly(s3_bucket: None):
    ts_str = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    upload("req-ts", [{"id": "req-ts", "timestamp": ts_str, "message": "msg"}])
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-ts")

    expected_ts = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts


def test_get_logs_uses_prefix_in_s3_key(s3_bucket: None):
    upload(
        "req-pfx",
        [{"id": "req-pfx", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}],
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-pfx")

    assert len(results) == 1


def test_get_logs_returns_empty_for_missing_object(s3_bucket: None):
    reader = build_reader()

    assert reader.get_logs("no-such-id") == []


def test_get_logs_returns_empty_for_missing_bucket():
    with mock_aws():
        s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
        reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="does-not-exist", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)

        assert reader.get_logs("req-1") == []


def test_get_logs_ignores_blank_lines_in_jsonl(s3_bucket: None):
    lines = '{"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "a"}\n\n{"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T01:00:00+00:00", "message": "b"}\n'
    compressed = gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))
    boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1").put_object(
        Bucket=TEST_BUCKET, Key=f"{TEST_PREFIX}/req-1.jsonl.gz", Body=compressed
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["a", "b"]

import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR

# Set required env vars before reporter_lambda is imported — its module-level
# get_audit_log_reader() call needs these to be present at import time.
_ = os.environ.setdefault(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "test-audit-table")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "test-bucket")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "test-prefix")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR, "memory")


import dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda as reporter_module  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AuditEntry,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
)  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda import lambda_handler  # noqa: E402


def make_get_event(id: str | None = "req-123") -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "pathParameters": {"id": id} if id is not None else None,
        "httpMethod": "GET",
    }


def make_mock_reader(entries: list[AuditEntry] | Exception) -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
    mock = MagicMock(spec=FallbackAuditLogReader)
    if isinstance(entries, Exception):
        mock.get_logs.side_effect = entries
    else:
        mock.get_logs.return_value = entries
    return mock


def make_context() -> MagicMock:
    ctx = MagicMock()
    return ctx


TS = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


def test_lambda_handler_returns_200_with_entries(monkeypatch):
    entries = [
        AuditEntry(id="req-123", timestamp=TS, message="created"),
        AuditEntry(
            id="req-123",
            timestamp=datetime(2026, 5, 7, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            message="processed",
        ),
    ]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 200
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-123"
    assert len(body["entries"]) == 2
    assert body["entries"][0]["message"] == "created"
    assert body["entries"][1]["message"] == "processed"
    assert body["entries"][0]["timestamp"] == "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"


def test_lambda_handler_returns_404_when_no_entries(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-unknown"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 404
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-unknown"
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_path_param_missing(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {"pathParameters": None}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_id_is_empty_string(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("  "), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_pathparameters_key_absent(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_500_on_reader_exception(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(RuntimeError("dynamo down"))
    )

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 500
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["error"] == "internal error"


def test_lambda_handler_response_has_json_content_type(monkeypatch):
    entries = [AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="msg")]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    assert response["headers"] == {"Content-Type": "application/json"}  # type: ignore[index]


def test_lambda_handler_entry_shape_matches_audit_entry_to_json(monkeypatch):
    entry = AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="test msg")
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([entry]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["entries"][0] == entry.to_json()

1	import json
2	from typing import Any
3	
4	from aws_lambda_powertools import Logger
5	from aws_lambda_powertools.tracing import Tracer
6	from aws_lambda_powertools.utilities.typing import LambdaContext
7	from dotenv import load_dotenv
8	
9	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
10	    FallbackAuditLogReader,
11	    get_audit_log_reader,
12	)
13	
14	# Load .env file, mostly useful for testing
15	_ = load_dotenv()
16	
17	logger = Logger()
18	tracer = Tracer()  # Sets service via POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME env var
19	
20	reader: FallbackAuditLogReader = get_audit_log_reader()
21	
22	
23	def _response(
24	    status_code: int,
25	    body: dict[str, Any],
26	) -> dict[str, object]:
27	    return {
28	        "statusCode": status_code,
29	        "headers": {"Content-Type": "application/json"},
30	        "body": json.dumps(body),
31	    }
32	
33	
34	@tracer.capture_lambda_handler
35	@logger.inject_lambda_context
36	def lambda_handler(
37	    event: dict[str, object],
38	    _context: LambdaContext,
39	) -> dict[str, object]:
40	    """Entrypoint for AWS Lambda that retrieves the audit log for an erasure id."""
41	    path_parameters = event.get("pathParameters") or {}
42	    erasure_id: object = (
43	        path_parameters.get("id") if isinstance(path_parameters, dict) else None
44	    )
45	
46	    if not isinstance(erasure_id, str) or not erasure_id.strip():
47	        return _response(400, {"error": "missing erasure id"})
48	
49	    try:
50	        entries = reader.get_logs(erasure_id)
51	    except Exception:
52	        logger.exception("Failed to retrieve audit log for %s", erasure_id)
53	        return _response(500, {"error": "internal error"})
54	
55	    if not entries:
56	        return _response(404, {"error": "no audit log found", "id": erasure_id})
57	
58	    return _response(
59	        200,
60	        {
61	            "id": erasure_id,
62	            "entries": [entry.to_json() for entry in entries],
63	        },
64	    )
65

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/skills/commit-message

## Context

Data Engineering changes typically touch ingestion pipelines, Airflow DAGs, AWS infrastructure, and source integrations. Commit messages should describe the user-visible intent and impact, not just restate low-level diff details.

## Instructions

1. Run `git diff origin/main..HEAD` to get the diff of the local branch against its remote base.
2. Derive the Jira key from the branch name when it matches `<JIRA-KEY>_<short-name>`.
   If the branch name does not include a Jira key, ask the user for one before generating the final message.
3. Write a one-line commit message in this format:

   ```text
   <JIRA-KEY>: <Imperative subject line>
   ```

4. Follow these rules:
   - Start the subject line with a capital letter.
   - Use imperative mood (command wording), not past tense or present participle.
   - Do not add a trailing period.
   - Do not add signatures or extra metadata.
   - Summarize intent and impact; do not just restate diff details.

## Examples

### Good

- `DA-1234: Fix use of deprecated method`
- `DA-5634: Add RtE cleaner component`

### Bad

- `DA-5634: Adding RtE cleaner component`  
  Reason: uses present participle instead of imperative mood.
- `Edit Common.scala to use method2 instead of method1 - DA-1234`  
  Reason: missing Jira key prefix format.
- `DA-1234: fix use of deprecated method`  
  Reason: subject line should start with a capital letter.
- `DA-1234: Fixed use of deprecated method`  
  Reason: uses past tense instead of imperative mood.
- `DA-5634: RtE cleaner component`  
  Reason: noun phrase instead of action.
- `DA-4634: Increase CPU request from 2 to 3`  
  Reason: too low-level and diff-like; prefer higher-level intent.
- `DA-6093: Add api-ingest tests to the CI pipeline.`  
  Reason: trailing period is not allowed.

185	    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
186	
187	    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
188	    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
189	    """
190	
191	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
192	        self.bucket: str = bucket
193	        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
194	        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
195	
196	    def _key_for(self, id: str) -> str:
197	        if self.prefix:
198	            return f"{self.prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
199	        return f"{id}.jsonl.gz"
200	
201	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
202	        key = self._key_for(id)
203	        try:
204	            response = self.s3.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)
205	        except ClientError as e:
206	            error_code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
207	            if error_code in ("NoSuchKey", "404", "NoSuchBucket"):
208	                return []
209	            raise
210	
211	        body_bytes: bytes = response["Body"].read()
212	        decompressed = gzip.decompress(body_bytes).decode("utf-8")
213	        entries: list[AuditEntry] = []
214	        for line in decompressed.splitlines():
215	            stripped = line.strip()
216	            if not stripped:
217	                continue
218	            entries.append(
219	                AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(stripped))
220	            )  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
221	        return entries

import gzip
import json
import logging
import os
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, ClassVar, Literal, override

import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    DynamoDBClient = Any

content.text: Now I have the real picture. The approach of using `dict[str, object]` causes a new error because `dict` is invariant. The correct fix is to use `Mapping[str, object]` for covariance, and use the proper DynamoDB type from the stubs. Let me look at what `AttributeValueTypeDef` looks like and use the right approach:

file.content:
import gzip
import json
import logging
import os
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, ClassVar, Literal, override

import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    DynamoDBClient = Any
    S3Client = Any

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

content.text: The cleanest solution matching the original intent is to import `AttributeValueTypeDef` for type-checking and use `Mapping` for covariance:

input.old_string:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, ClassVar, Literal, override

import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    DynamoDBClient = Any
    S3Client = Any
input.new_string:
from collections.abc import Mapping
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, ClassVar, Literal, override

import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient
    from types_boto3_dynamodb.type_defs import AttributeValueTypeDef
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    AttributeValueTypeDef = Any
    DynamoDBClient = Any
    S3Client = Any

content.thinking:
The issue is that `AttributeValueTypeDef` is a TypedDict and accessing items inside it with string keys that aren't in the TypedDict schema is causing "Could not access item in TypedDict" errors. The `AttributeValueTypeDef` has specific keys like `"S"`, `"N"`, etc. but accessing `item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD]["S"]` — where `item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD]` returns an `AttributeValueTypeDef`, and then we're accessing `["S"]` on it — that `"S"` access should be valid as `"S"` is a key in `AttributeValueTypeDef`.


Actually, I think the problem might be that the type checker doesn't recognize `"S"` as a valid key even though it's defined in the TypedDict schema, possibly because of how the TypedDict is structured with `total=False`. The issue is that `item` is typed as `Mapping[str, AttributeValueTypeDef]`, so accessing it with `AuditEntry.ID_FIELD` (a `ClassVar[Literal["id"]]`) should work and return an `AttributeValueTypeDef`, then accessing the `"S"` key on that should give me a `str`. But the error message suggests the type checker is having trouble with the nested TypedDict access—I need to verify what `AttributeValueTypeDef["S"]` actually resolves to.

Looking back at the original code, it was already using `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` comments to suppress these errors, so the approach was to work around the type checker's limitations rather than fix the underlying types. Let me see what the original implementation looked like... I'm seeing a list of pyright warnings across multiple lines in the audit_logging.py file, mostly about `Any` types and unnecessary ignore comments. The pre-commit hook failed with exit code 1, which suggests it's treating these warnings as errors rather than just flagging them.

Looking at what changed, it seems black's reformatting shifted some code around, which made certain `# pyright: ignore` comments become unnecessary while also introducing new `reportAny` warnings on lines that weren't flagged before. The original code had these ignore comments in place to suppress the `reportExplicitAny` and `reportAny` warnings, but after the formatting pass, the line numbers and context shifted, breaking the alignment between the warnings and their corresponding ignore directives. Looking at the pyright warnings, the ignore comment got separated from the actual line causing the issue when black reformatted the function signature across multiple lines. The warning about `Any` is on the parameter line, but the ignore directive ended up on the closing parenthesis line instead, so pyright is now complaining that the ignore is unnecessary where it's placed. move the ignore comment to line 171 where the warning actually fires. Looking at the existing code, I see `get_dynamodb_audit_logger` has the same pattern and generates similar warnings — these might be pre-existing issues that were already in the codebase before the recent changes. The `dynamodb` client initialization at line 259 in `get_dynamodb_audit_logger` likely has the same type warning, and I'm wondering if basedpyright treats warnings as failures since the pre-commit hook exited with code 1.

Looking more carefully at the error output, I see `reportUnnecessaryTypeIgnoreComment` is the culprit — those `# pyright: ignore` comments became redundant after Black reformatted the code, and that's what's causing the exit code 1. So the real issue is that Black's reformatting made some of the type ignore comments unnecessary.

The fix involves repositioning those comments to the correct lines where they're actually needed, or removing them entirely if the underlying issues have been resolved. I should check what the current basedpyright output shows after my changes to see which warnings are still present. Looking at the error output from basedpyright, I see the ignore comments on line 174 are unnecessary—they're suppressing rules that aren't actually being triggered. I need to remove those redundant ignores and focus on the actual TypedDict access error and the unknown type warning for dynamodb.

The warnings about unknown variable types on lines 262 and 267 are puzzling since those functions existed before my changes, yet pre-commit was passing. Maybe basedpyright's configuration changed or it's now treating unknown variable types as warnings instead of errors, which would explain why the pre-commit is failing now when it wasn't before.

Looking at the actual error-level issues versus warnings, the real problem is the TypedDict access error I introduced with my `Mapping[str, AttributeValueTypeDef]` change. I need to revert that back to `dict[str, Any]` to fix the actual errors, while the unnecessary type ignore comments are just warnings that might be contributing to the exit code failure.

For the `_item_to_entry` function, I'll keep `dict[str, Any]` on the parameter line with an ignore comment there, then place individual ignore comments on each line where we're accessing dictionary items. For the S3 reader's `json.loads` call, I need to add the appropriate ignore comment to suppress the type checking warning on that line as well.

Regarding the `dynamodb` and `s3` unknown variable type warnings on lines 262 and 267 — these are likely pre-existing issues from the `get_dynamodb_audit_logger` function that was already in the codebase, so they probably weren't causing failures before. The actual new failures I introduced are the `reportUnnecessaryTypeIgnoreComment` warnings that need addressing. Looking at the actual error output, I see warnings from basedpyright about `reportExplicitAny` and `reportUnnecessaryTypeIgnoreComment` on lines 171-172. The exit code 1 suggests basedpyright is configured to fail on warnings, not just errors. Since the `get_dynamodb_audit_logger` function existed before these changes and would have triggered the same `reportUnknownVariableType` warning, the fact that it didn't fail the hook before means either that rule wasn't enabled or the configuration has changed.

The real culprit is the misplaced ignore comments introduced by black's reformatting. The `# pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]` comment ended up on the wrong line after the function signature was split across multiple lines. I need to revert the complex type annotation change and go back to using `dict[str, Any]` with the ignore comment placed correctly on the line where the `Any` type actually appears.

The same issue happens with the timestamp access and S3 code—the ignore comments are on the closing parenthesis lines instead of the lines where the actual warnings fire, so I just need to move them to the correct positions.

Since black has already reformatted the file, it won't change again unless there are other modifications, so I can work with what's there now. content.text: These errors say that `FallbackAuditLogReader`, `get_audit_log_reader`, `AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET`, `AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX`, `AuditEntry`, and `S3AuditLogReader` are unknown import symbols from `audit_logging`. This means the `prek` check is running against the _stashed_ (original master) version of `audit_logging.py`, not the modified one. Let me check:

content.text: The symbols exist. The `prek` tool applies changes to a temp copy — the "Restored working tree" message means it patched and then restored. The errors are because prek saw the staged but unmodified version. Let me stage the current `audit_logging.py` and run prek again:

The real problem is that black's reformatting moved the `# pyright: ignore` comments to the wrong lines—they ended up on the closing parenthesis and the return type instead of where the actual `Any` types are. Line 171 has the `dict[str, Any]` parameter that needs the ignore comment, but it's currently on line 172 (unnecessary there). Similarly, the ignore on line 176 was shifted by black when it should be on line 175 where the `reportAny` error actually occurs.

The solution is either to keep the function signature on a single line so the ignore comment stays with `dict[str, Any]`, or find a way to place the ignores on the correct lines despite black's formatting.

Both versions are 1.39.x so the behavior should be similar. The issue is that black reformats the multi-line expression so the `# pyright: ignore` comment ends up on a different line than where the `Any` value is. Let me look at the precise pyright error positions:

- Line 171: `cls, item: dict[str, Any]` — `Any` here triggers `reportExplicitAny`
- Line 172: `] -> AuditEntry:  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]` — this is marked as unnecessary because the ignore is on a different line than the `Any`

The issue is that the ignore comment on line 172 doesn't cover line 171. The fix is to put the ignore comment on the same line as the `Any`:

content.text:
Black keeps splitting the function signature because the line `def _item_to_entry(cls, item: dict[str, Any]) -> AuditEntry:  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]` is over 88 characters. When black splits it, the `# pyright: ignore` ends up on line 172 (with `-> AuditEntry:`) but the `Any` is on line 171. That's why pyright says the ignore is "unnecessary" — the warning is on line 171, not 172.

The fundamental tension: black splits the line, which moves the `# pyright: ignore` to a different line than the `Any`. The solution is to add the ignore on the parameter line itself — where `Any` actually is:

content.text:
`object` is too restrictive — it causes errors when passed to typed params. Let me revert back to `dict[str, Any]` for the stub data and use pyright ignores on those specific lines. The issue is that `dict` is invariant — `dict[str, dict[str, str]]` can't be assigned to `dict[str, object]`.

The correct approach for test stubs is to keep using `Any` but suppress the warning with `# pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]` on those specific type annotation lines:

input.old_string: from typing import cast
input.new_string: from typing import Any, cast

The pattern is the same issue: `Any` is on line 30, 36 etc., but `# pyright: ignore` ends up on 31, 37. The ignores need to be on the line with `Any`, not the closing `) -> ...:` line.

The solution is to move `# pyright: ignore` onto the line with the `Any` annotation, before the closing paren:

content.text:
Black reformatted again and put the ignore back on the wrong line. This is a whack-a-mole issue — black keeps reformatting `list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None` into a multi-line form that splits the `Any` from the ignore.

The proper solution is to use a `TypeAlias` so the line stays short enough that black doesn't split it:

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    def __init__(
        self, pages: list[dict[str, Any]]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
    ) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]

    def paginate(
        self, **kwargs: Any  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
        self._last_kwargs = kwargs
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    def __init__(
        self,
        query_pages: (
            list[dict[str, Any]] | None
        ) = None,  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
    ) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator

Only lines 264 and 264 remain. The `boto3.client("dynamodb")` warning — this is `get_dynamodb_audit_logger` which was already in the original code on master. The hook was not triggered before because those files weren't being committed. Now that I'm staging `audit_logging.py`, the hook runs on it.

The warning is `reportUnknownVariableType` for `dynamodb` and `reportUnknownMemberType` for `boto3.client`. The fix is to add `# pyright: ignore` on line 264:

51	class AuditLogger(ABC):
52	    """Base interface for audit logging"""
53	
54	    @abstractmethod
55	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
56	        """
57	        Log an audit log message.
58	
59	        The id should be the same as an erasure requests id
60	
61	        The message can be any free text. You should avoid storing raw personal
62	        information in messages, and make use of anonymisation functions.
63	
64	        The timestamp is optional and if absent will be set to now using the
65	        UTC timezone. All datetimes given will be coereced to UTC.
66	        """
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    @abstractmethod
70	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
71	        """
72	        Retrieve all audit log entries for the given erasure id.
73	
74	        Entries are returned in chronological order (oldest first). Returns
75	        an empty list if no entries exist for the id.
76	        """
77	        raise NotImplementedError()
78

80	class NoOpAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
81	    """Audit logger implementation that does nothing"""
82	
83	    @override
84	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
85	        logger.warning("Audit message not saved anywhere: %s", message)
86	        pass
87	
88	    @override
89	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
90	        return []

93	class InMemoryAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
94	    """Example implementation of audit logging by storing messages in memory"""
95	
96	    def __init__(self) -> None:
97	        super().__init__()
98	        self.memory_log: list[tuple[tuple[str, datetime], str]] = []
99	
100	    @override
101	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
102	        if timestamp is None:
103	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
104	        else:
105	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
106	        self.memory_log.append(((id, timestamp), message))
107	
108	    @override
109	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
110	        entries = [
111	            AuditEntry(id=entry_id, timestamp=ts, message=msg)
112	            for ((entry_id, ts), msg) in self.memory_log
113	            if entry_id == id
114	        ]
115	        entries.sort(key=lambda e: e.timestamp)
116	        return entries

119	class DynamoDBAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
120	    """DynamoDB backed audit logging"""
121	
122	    def __init__(self, table_name: str, dynamodb: DynamoDBClient) -> None:
123	        super().__init__()
124	        self.table_name: str = table_name
125	        self.dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = dynamodb
126	
127	    @override
128	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
129	        if timestamp is None:
130	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
131	        else:
132	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
133	        item = self._create_item(id, message, timestamp)
134	        _ = self.dynamodb.put_item(
135	            TableName=self.table_name,
136	            Item=item,
137	            ReturnValues="NONE",
138	        )
139	        logger.info("Audit Message sent: %s", message)
140	
141	    @override
142	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
143	        paginator = self.dynamodb.get_paginator("query")
144	        pages = paginator.paginate(
145	            TableName=self.table_name,
146	            KeyConditionExpression="#id = :id",
147	            ExpressionAttributeNames={"#id": AuditEntry.ID_FIELD},
148	            ExpressionAttributeValues={":id": {"S": id}},
149	            ScanIndexForward=True,
150	        )
151	        results: list[AuditEntry] = []
152	        for page in pages:
153	            if "Items" not in page:
154	                continue
155	            for item in page["Items"]:
156	                results.append(self._item_to_entry(item))
157	        return results
158	
159	    @classmethod
160	    def _create_item(cls, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime):
161	        timestamp_ms: int = int(timestamp.timestamp() * 1000)
162	        item = {
163	            AuditEntry.ID_FIELD: {"S": id},
164	            AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: {"N": str(timestamp_ms)},
165	            AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD: {"S": message},
166	        }
167	        return item
168	
169	    @classmethod
170	    def _item_to_entry(
171	        cls, item: dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
172	    ) -> AuditEntry:
173	        id_value: str = item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD]["S"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
174	        timestamp_ms: int = int(
175	            item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]["N"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
176	        )
177	        message: str = item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD]["S"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
178	        timestamp: datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(
179	            timestamp_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc
180	        )
181	        return AuditEntry(id=id_value, timestamp=timestamp, message=message)
182

184	class S3AuditLogReader:
185	    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
186	
187	    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
188	    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
189	    """
190	
191	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
192	        self.bucket: str = bucket
193	        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")

Black split these lines too. The warning for line 264 (`reportUnknownVariableType`) is about the variable declaration `dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = ...`, and the `"dynamodb"` string is on line 265. The ignore on line 266 is the closing paren, which doesn't cover lines 264/265.

This is the same pattern. Let me use a helper approach — define a typed function for client creation, or just put the full call on one line and accept that it will be over 88 chars (pyright will still run, just black won't like it — but actually black allows `# type: ignore`-style comments to preserve long lines).

Actually, the cleanest solution is to use `cast()` to annotate the result, since that works in one line:

The warnings are:
- Line 264: `reportUnknownVariableType` (the `dynamodb` variable)  
- Line 264: `reportUnknownMemberType` (the `boto3.client` call)

Since the annotation is `dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = ...`, the issue is that `boto3.client("dynamodb")` returns an unknown type that can't be cast to `DynamoDBClient`. The ignore needs to be on line 264, not the closing paren.

Let me rewrite these two functions to keep everything on one line by not using a multi-line call:

Black insists on splitting these long lines and moving the ignores to the closing paren. This is fundamentally incompatible with pyright's requirement that ignores be on the same line as the warning.

The solution is to use `noqa` style or a different approach entirely. Since `boto3.client("dynamodb")` is what returns the untyped value, and `DynamoDBClient` is only defined at TYPE_CHECKING time, I need to restructure. The right approach: use `cast` to silence the warning without needing an inline comment on a long line:

import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR

# Set required env vars before reporter_lambda is imported — its module-level
# get_audit_log_reader() call needs these to be present at import time.
_ = os.environ.setdefault(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "test-audit-table")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "test-bucket")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "test-prefix")

import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR

# Set required env vars before reporter_lambda is imported — its module-level
# get_audit_log_reader() call needs these to be present at import time.
_ = os.environ.setdefault(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "test-audit-table")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "test-bucket")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "test-prefix")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR, "memory")


import dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda as reporter_module  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AuditEntry,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
)  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda import lambda_handler  # noqa: E402


def make_get_event(id: str | None = "req-123") -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "pathParameters": {"id": id} if id is not None else None,
        "httpMethod": "GET",
    }


def make_mock_reader(entries: list[AuditEntry] | Exception) -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
    mock = MagicMock(spec=FallbackAuditLogReader)
    if isinstance(entries, Exception):
        mock.get_logs.side_effect = entries
    else:
        mock.get_logs.return_value = entries
    return mock


def make_context() -> MagicMock:
    ctx = MagicMock()
    return ctx


TS = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


def test_lambda_handler_returns_200_with_entries(monkeypatch):
    entries = [
        AuditEntry(id="req-123", timestamp=TS, message="created"),
        AuditEntry(
            id="req-123",
            timestamp=datetime(2026, 5, 7, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            message="processed",
        ),
    ]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 200
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-123"
    assert len(body["entries"]) == 2
    assert body["entries"][0]["message"] == "created"
    assert body["entries"][1]["message"] == "processed"
    assert body["entries"][0]["timestamp"] == "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"


def test_lambda_handler_returns_404_when_no_entries(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-unknown"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 404
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-unknown"
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_path_param_missing(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {"pathParameters": None}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_id_is_empty_string(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("  "), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_pathparameters_key_absent(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_500_on_reader_exception(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(RuntimeError("dynamo down"))
    )

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 500
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["error"] == "internal error"


def test_lambda_handler_response_has_json_content_type(monkeypatch):
    entries = [AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="msg")]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    assert response["headers"] == {"Content-Type": "application/json"}  # type: ignore[index]


def test_lambda_handler_entry_shape_matches_audit_entry_to_json(monkeypatch):
    entry = AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="test msg")
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([entry]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["entries"][0] == entry.to_json()

import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from pytest import MonkeyPatch

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR

# Set required env vars before reporter_lambda is imported — its module-level
# get_audit_log_reader() call needs these to be present at import time.
_ = os.environ.setdefault(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "test-audit-table")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "test-bucket")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "test-prefix")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR, "memory")


import dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda as reporter_module  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AuditEntry,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
)  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda import lambda_handler  # noqa: E402


def make_get_event(id: str | None = "req-123") -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "pathParameters": {"id": id} if id is not None else None,
        "httpMethod": "GET",
    }


def make_mock_reader(entries: list[AuditEntry] | Exception) -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
    mock = MagicMock(spec=FallbackAuditLogReader)
    if isinstance(entries, Exception):
        mock.get_logs.side_effect = entries
    else:
        mock.get_logs.return_value = entries
    return mock


def make_context() -> MagicMock:
    ctx = MagicMock()
    return ctx


TS = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


def test_lambda_handler_returns_200_with_entries(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    entries = [
        AuditEntry(id="req-123", timestamp=TS, message="created"),
        AuditEntry(
            id="req-123",
            timestamp=datetime(2026, 5, 7, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            message="processed",
        ),
    ]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 200
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-123"
    assert len(body["entries"]) == 2
    assert body["entries"][0]["message"] == "created"
    assert body["entries"][1]["message"] == "processed"
    assert body["entries"][0]["timestamp"] == "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"


def test_lambda_handler_returns_404_when_no_entries(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-unknown"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 404
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-unknown"
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_path_param_missing(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {"pathParameters": None}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

content.text:
The `lambda_handler` returns `dict[str, object]` but pyright is seeing it as `Any` because the module-level code creates `reader` at import time using `get_audit_log_reader()` which involves `boto3.client()`. The `response` from `lambda_handler` is being typed as `Any`. 

This is cascading from the module-level `reader = get_audit_log_reader()` and how pyright understands the module. The root is that pyright infers `lambda_handler` returns `Any` due to the unresolved `cast("DynamoDBClient", ...)`.

This is getting complex — the root cause is the `cast` pattern I introduced. Let me check if `lambda_handler` itself has a defined return type:

The `lambda_handler` is annotated to return `dict[str, object]`. The issue is that the `cast` in `get_dynamodb_audit_logger` is causing the whole chain to be `Any`.

Let me take a step back and check what the pre-existing tests look like — the project was working before my changes. The existing `test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` didn't have these issues. The warnings in `test_reporter_lambda.py` about `response` being `Any` are likely because `lambda_handler` is being called through a module that's `Any`-typed at import time.

Let me check how many total warnings remain across all files:

content.text:
The `response` is `Any`. This happens because `make_context()` returns `MagicMock`, and pyright can't match it to `LambdaContext`. When a function argument doesn't match, pyright might fall through to an overload that returns `Any`, or it could just be that the strict `reportAny` also flags cases where the return value is obtained from a function call where there were type-check failures in the arguments.

Actually, `lambda_handler` takes `dict[str, object], LambdaContext` — if `make_context()` returns `MagicMock` (not a `LambdaContext`), pyright may not be able to determine the return type and falls back to `Any`.

The simplest fix is to type `make_context()` to return `LambdaContext`:

import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from pytest import MonkeyPatch

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR

# Set required env vars before reporter_lambda is imported — its module-level
# get_audit_log_reader() call needs these to be present at import time.
_ = os.environ.setdefault(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "test-audit-table")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "test-bucket")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "test-prefix")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR, "memory")


import dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda as reporter_module  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AuditEntry,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
)  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda import lambda_handler  # noqa: E402


def make_get_event(id: str | None = "req-123") -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "pathParameters": {"id": id} if id is not None else None,
        "httpMethod": "GET",
    }


def make_mock_reader(entries: list[AuditEntry] | Exception) -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
    mock = MagicMock(spec=FallbackAuditLogReader)
    if isinstance(entries, Exception):
        mock.get_logs.side_effect = entries  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    else:
        mock.get_logs.return_value = entries  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    return cast(FallbackAuditLogReader, mock)


def make_context() -> MagicMock:
    ctx = MagicMock()
    return ctx


TS = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


def test_lambda_handler_returns_200_with_entries(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    entries = [
        AuditEntry(id="req-123", timestamp=TS, message="created"),
        AuditEntry(
            id="req-123",
            timestamp=datetime(2026, 5, 7, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            message="processed",
        ),
    ]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 200
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-123"
    assert len(body["entries"]) == 2
    assert body["entries"][0]["message"] == "created"
    assert body["entries"][1]["message"] == "processed"
    assert body["entries"][0]["timestamp"] == "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"


def test_lambda_handler_returns_404_when_no_entries(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-unknown"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 404
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-unknown"
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_path_param_missing(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {"pathParameters": None}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_id_is_empty_string(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("  "), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_pathparameters_key_absent(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_500_on_reader_exception(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(RuntimeError("dynamo down"))
    )

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 500
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["error"] == "internal error"


def test_lambda_handler_response_has_json_content_type(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    entries = [AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="msg")]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    assert response["headers"] == {"Content-Type": "application/json"}  # type: ignore[index]


def test_lambda_handler_entry_shape_matches_audit_entry_to_json(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    entry = AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="test msg")
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([entry]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["entries"][0] == entry.to_json()

Actually wait — `cast` in Python typing _narrows_ the type to the specified type. `cast("FallbackAuditLogReader", ...)` should return `FallbackAuditLogReader`, not `Any`.

20	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

23	@dataclass(frozen=True)
24	class AuditEntry:
25	    """A single audit log entry for an erasure request."""
26	
27	    ID_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["id"]] = "id"
28	    TIMESTAMP_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["timestamp"]] = "timestamp"
29	    MESSAGE_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["message"]] = "message"
30	
31	    id: str
32	    timestamp: datetime
33	    message: str
34	
35	    def to_json(self) -> dict[str, str]:
36	        return {
37	            self.ID_FIELD: self.id,
38	            self.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: self.timestamp.isoformat(),
39	            self.MESSAGE_FIELD: self.message,
40	        }
41	
42	    @classmethod
43	    def from_json(cls, data: dict[str, str]) -> "AuditEntry":
44	        return cls(
45	            id=data[cls.ID_FIELD],
46	            timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(data[cls.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]),
47	            message=data[cls.MESSAGE_FIELD],
48	        )

51	class AuditLogger(ABC):
52	    """Base interface for audit logging"""
53	
54	    @abstractmethod
55	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
56	        """
57	        Log an audit log message.
58	
59	        The id should be the same as an erasure requests id
60	
61	        The message can be any free text. You should avoid storing raw personal
62	        information in messages, and make use of anonymisation functions.
63	
64	        The timestamp is optional and if absent will be set to now using the
65	        UTC timezone. All datetimes given will be coereced to UTC.
66	        """
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    @abstractmethod
70	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
71	        """
72	        Retrieve all audit log entries for the given erasure id.
73	
74	        Entries are returned in chronological order (oldest first). Returns
75	        an empty list if no entries exist for the id.
76	        """
77	        raise NotImplementedError()
78	
79	
80	class NoOpAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
81	    """Audit logger implementation that does nothing"""
82	
83	    @override
84	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
85	        logger.warning("Audit message not saved anywhere: %s", message)
86	        pass
87	
88	    @override
89	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
90	        return []
91	
92	
93	class InMemoryAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
94	    """Example implementation of audit logging by storing messages in memory"""
95	
96	    def __init__(self) -> None:
97	        super().__init__()
98	        self.memory_log: list[tuple[tuple[str, datetime], str]] = []
99	
100	    @override
101	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
102	        if timestamp is None:
103	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
104	        else:
105	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
106	        self.memory_log.append(((id, timestamp), message))
107	
108	    @override
109	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
110	        entries = [
111	            AuditEntry(id=entry_id, timestamp=ts, message=msg)
112	            for ((entry_id, ts), msg) in self.memory_log
113	            if entry_id == id
114	        ]
115	        entries.sort(key=lambda e: e.timestamp)
116	        return entries
117

119	class DynamoDBAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
120	    """DynamoDB backed audit logging"""
121	
122	    def __init__(self, table_name: str, dynamodb: DynamoDBClient) -> None:
123	        super().__init__()
124	        self.table_name: str = table_name
125	        self.dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = dynamodb
126	
127	    @override
128	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
129	        if timestamp is None:
130	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
131	        else:
132	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
133	        item = self._create_item(id, message, timestamp)
134	        _ = self.dynamodb.put_item(
135	            TableName=self.table_name,
136	            Item=item,
137	            ReturnValues="NONE",
138	        )
139	        logger.info("Audit Message sent: %s", message)
140	
141	    @override
142	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
143	        paginator = self.dynamodb.get_paginator("query")
144	        pages = paginator.paginate(
145	            TableName=self.table_name,
146	            KeyConditionExpression="#id = :id",
147	            ExpressionAttributeNames={"#id": AuditEntry.ID_FIELD},
148	            ExpressionAttributeValues={":id": {"S": id}},
149	            ScanIndexForward=True,
150	        )
151	        results: list[AuditEntry] = []
152	        for page in pages:
153	            if "Items" not in page:
154	                continue
155	            for item in page["Items"]:
156	                results.append(self._item_to_entry(item))
157	        return results
158	
159	    @classmethod
160	    def _create_item(cls, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime):
161	        timestamp_ms: int = int(timestamp.timestamp() * 1000)
162	        item = {
163	            AuditEntry.ID_FIELD: {"S": id},
164	            AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: {"N": str(timestamp_ms)},
165	            AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD: {"S": message},
166	        }
167	        return item
168	
169	    @classmethod
170	    def _item_to_entry(
171	        cls, item: dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
172	    ) -> AuditEntry:
173	        id_value: str = item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD]["S"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
174	        timestamp_ms: int = int(
175	            item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]["N"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
176	        )
177	        message: str = item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD]["S"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
178	        timestamp: datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(
179	            timestamp_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc
180	        )
181	        return AuditEntry(id=id_value, timestamp=timestamp, message=message)
