184	class S3AuditLogReader:
185	    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
186	
187	    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
188	    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
189	    """
190	
191	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
192	        self.bucket: str = bucket
193	        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
194	        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
195	
196	    def _key_for(self, id: str) -> str:
197	        if self.prefix:
198	            return f"{self.prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
199	        return f"{id}.jsonl.gz"
200	
201	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
202	        key = self._key_for(id)
203	        try:
204	            response = self.s3.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)
205	        except ClientError as e:
206	            error_code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
207	            if error_code in ("NoSuchKey", "404", "NoSuchBucket"):
208	                return []
209	            raise
210	
211	        body_bytes: bytes = response["Body"].read()
212	        decompressed = gzip.decompress(body_bytes).decode("utf-8")
213	        entries: list[AuditEntry] = []
214	        for line in decompressed.splitlines():
215	            stripped = line.strip()
216	            if not stripped:
217	                continue
218	            entries.append(
219	                AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(stripped))  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
220	            )
221	        return entries
222

51	class AuditLogger(ABC):
52	    """Base interface for audit logging"""
53	
54	    @abstractmethod
55	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
56	        """
57	        Log an audit log message.
58	
59	        The id should be the same as an erasure requests id
60	
61	        The message can be any free text. You should avoid storing raw personal
62	        information in messages, and make use of anonymisation functions.
63	
64	        The timestamp is optional and if absent will be set to now using the
65	        UTC timezone. All datetimes given will be coereced to UTC.
66	        """
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68

70	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
71	        """

73	
74	        Entries are returned in chronological order (oldest first). Returns
75	        an empty list if no entries exist for the id.
76	        """
77	        raise NotImplementedError()

104	        else:

119	class DynamoDBAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
120	    """DynamoDB backed audit logging"""
121	
122	    def __init__(self, table_name: str, dynamodb: DynamoDBClient) -> None:
123	        super().__init__()
124	        self.table_name: str = table_name
125	        self.dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = dynamodb
126	
127	    @override
128	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
129	        if timestamp is None:
130	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
131	        else:
132	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
133	        item = self._create_item(id, message, timestamp)
134	        _ = self.dynamodb.put_item(
135	            TableName=self.table_name,
136	            Item=item,
137	            ReturnValues="NONE",
138	        )
139	        logger.info("Audit Message sent: %s", message)
140	
141	    @override
142	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
143	        paginator = self.dynamodb.get_paginator("query")
144	        pages = paginator.paginate(
145	            TableName=self.table_name,
146	            KeyConditionExpression="#id = :id",
147	            ExpressionAttributeNames={"#id": AuditEntry.ID_FIELD},
148	            ExpressionAttributeValues={":id": {"S": id}},
149	            ScanIndexForward=True,
150	        )
151	        results: list[AuditEntry] = []
152	        for page in pages:
153	            if "Items" not in page:
154	                continue
155	            for item in page["Items"]:
156	                results.append(self._item_to_entry(item))
157	        return results
158	
159	    @classmethod
160	    def _create_item(cls, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime):
161	        timestamp_ms: int = int(timestamp.timestamp() * 1000)
162	        item = {
163	            AuditEntry.ID_FIELD: {"S": id},
164	            AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: {"N": str(timestamp_ms)},
165	            AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD: {"S": message},
166	        }
167	        return item
168	
169	    @classmethod
170	    def _item_to_entry(
171	        cls, item: dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
172	    ) -> AuditEntry:
173	        id_value: str = item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD]["S"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
174	        timestamp_ms: int = int(
175	            item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]["N"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
176	        )
177	        message: str = item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD]["S"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
178	        timestamp: datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(
179	            timestamp_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc
180	        )
181	        return AuditEntry(id=id_value, timestamp=timestamp, message=message)

184	class S3AuditLogReader:
185	    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
186	
187	    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
188	    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
189	    """
190	
191	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
192	        self.bucket: str = bucket
193	        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
194	        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
195	
196	    def _key_for(self, id: str) -> str:
197	        if self.prefix:
198	            return f"{self.prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
199	        return f"{id}.jsonl.gz"
200	
201	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
202	        key = self._key_for(id)
203	        try:
204	            response = self.s3.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)
205	        except ClientError as e:
206	            error_code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
207	            if error_code in ("NoSuchKey", "404", "NoSuchBucket"):
208	                return []
209	            raise
210	
211	        body_bytes: bytes = response["Body"].read()
212	        decompressed = gzip.decompress(body_bytes).decode("utf-8")
213	        entries: list[AuditEntry] = []
214	        for line in decompressed.splitlines():
215	            stripped = line.strip()
216	            if not stripped:
217	                continue
218	            entries.append(
219	                AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(stripped))  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
220	            )
221	        return entries

content.text: The `response` is still `Any` from `lambda_handler`. Let me understand why — pyright should know that `lambda_handler` returns `dict[str, object]`:

content.content:

57

content.text:
The issue is the decorators: `@tracer.capture_lambda_handler` and `@logger.inject_lambda_context`. These decorators from `aws_lambda_powertools` likely return `Any` because their types are `Any` in the type stubs. When a decorator returns `Any`, the decorated function's return type becomes `Any`.

That's why `response` is `Any` — the decorators lose the type information.

The fix is to use `cast` when calling `lambda_handler` in the tests, or to add a type annotation to the `response` variable:

The cleanest solution is to add `response: dict[str, object] = ...` explicit type annotations. But since there are many test functions, let me add a helper:

Actually, the simplest fix: add the explicit type annotation on each `response` line. But that's repetitive. A better approach: define a typed wrapper that calls `lambda_handler` and casts the result:

import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from aws_lambda_powertools.utilities.typing import LambdaContext
from pytest import MonkeyPatch

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR

# Set required env vars before reporter_lambda is imported — its module-level
# get_audit_log_reader() call needs these to be present at import time.
_ = os.environ.setdefault(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "test-audit-table")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "test-bucket")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "test-prefix")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR, "memory")


import dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda as reporter_module  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AuditEntry,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
)  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda import lambda_handler  # noqa: E402


def make_get_event(id: str | None = "req-123") -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "pathParameters": {"id": id} if id is not None else None,
        "httpMethod": "GET",
    }


def make_mock_reader(entries: list[AuditEntry] | Exception) -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
    mock = MagicMock(spec=FallbackAuditLogReader)
    if isinstance(entries, Exception):
        mock.get_logs.side_effect = entries  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    else:
        mock.get_logs.return_value = entries  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    return cast(FallbackAuditLogReader, mock)


def make_context() -> LambdaContext:
    return cast(LambdaContext, MagicMock())


TS = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


def test_lambda_handler_returns_200_with_entries(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    entries = [
        AuditEntry(id="req-123", timestamp=TS, message="created"),
        AuditEntry(
            id="req-123",
            timestamp=datetime(2026, 5, 7, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            message="processed",
        ),
    ]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 200
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-123"
    assert len(body["entries"]) == 2
    assert body["entries"][0]["message"] == "created"
    assert body["entries"][1]["message"] == "processed"
    assert body["entries"][0]["timestamp"] == "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"


def test_lambda_handler_returns_404_when_no_entries(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-unknown"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 404
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-unknown"
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_path_param_missing(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {"pathParameters": None}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_id_is_empty_string(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("  "), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_pathparameters_key_absent(
    monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch,
):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_500_on_reader_exception(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(RuntimeError("dynamo down"))
    )

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 500
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["error"] == "internal error"


def test_lambda_handler_response_has_json_content_type(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    entries = [AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="msg")]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    assert response["headers"] == {"Content-Type": "application/json"}  # type: ignore[index]


def test_lambda_handler_entry_shape_matches_audit_entry_to_json(
    monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch,
):
    entry = AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="test msg")
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([entry]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["entries"][0] == entry.to_json()

import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from aws_lambda_powertools.utilities.typing import LambdaContext
from pytest import MonkeyPatch

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR

# Set required env vars before reporter_lambda is imported — its module-level
# get_audit_log_reader() call needs these to be present at import time.
_ = os.environ.setdefault(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "test-audit-table")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "test-bucket")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "test-prefix")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR, "memory")


import dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda as reporter_module  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AuditEntry,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,

from datetime import datetime, timezone

content.text: The warning is on line 41 — "partially unknown" for `path_parameters`. The `raw_params` is `object`, and `isinstance(raw_params, dict)` narrows it to `dict[Unknown, Unknown]`. Let me check what pyright says precisely:

content.content: /Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/reporter_lambda.py:41:5 - warning: Type of "path_parameters" is partially unknown

toolUseResult.stdout: /Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/reporter_lambda.py:41:5 - warning: Type of "path_parameters" is partially unknown

content.text: The `path_parameters: dict[str, object] = (raw_params if isinstance(raw_params, dict) else {})` — when `isinstance(raw_params, dict)` passes, the type becomes `dict[Unknown, Unknown]`, which is "partially unknown" when assigned to `dict[str, object]`. Adding an explicit ignore:

import gzip
import json
from collections.abc import Generator
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Literal

import boto3
import pytest
from moto import mock_aws

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import S3AuditLogReader

TEST_BUCKET: Literal["test-audit-bucket"] = "test-audit-bucket"
TEST_PREFIX: Literal["audit-logs"] = "audit-logs"


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


@pytest.fixture
def s3_bucket() -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    with mock_aws():
        s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
        s3.create_bucket(
            Bucket=TEST_BUCKET,
            CreateBucketConfiguration={"LocationConstraint": "eu-west-1"},
        )
        yield


def build_reader() -> S3AuditLogReader:
    s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
    return S3AuditLogReader(bucket=TEST_BUCKET, prefix=TEST_PREFIX, s3_client=s3)


def upload(id: str, entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> None:
    s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
    key = f"{TEST_PREFIX}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
    s3.put_object(Bucket=TEST_BUCKET, Key=key, Body=_make_gzipped_jsonl(entries))


def test_get_logs_returns_entries_in_file_order(s3_bucket: None):
    ts1 = "2026-05-07T10:00:00+00:00"
    ts2 = "2026-05-07T11:00:00+00:00"
    ts3 = "2026-05-07T12:00:00+00:00"
    upload(
        "req-1",
        [
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts1, "message": "first"},
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts2, "message": "second"},
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts3, "message": "third"},
        ],
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 3
    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["first", "second", "third"]


def test_get_logs_parses_timestamps_correctly(s3_bucket: None):
    ts_str = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    upload("req-ts", [{"id": "req-ts", "timestamp": ts_str, "message": "msg"}])
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-ts")

    expected_ts = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts


def test_get_logs_uses_prefix_in_s3_key(s3_bucket: None):
    upload(
        "req-pfx",
        [{"id": "req-pfx", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}],
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-pfx")

    assert len(results) == 1


def test_get_logs_returns_empty_for_missing_object(s3_bucket: None):
    reader = build_reader()

    assert reader.get_logs("no-such-id") == []


def test_get_logs_returns_empty_for_missing_bucket():
    with mock_aws():
        s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
        reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="does-not-exist", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)

        assert reader.get_logs("req-1") == []


def test_get_logs_ignores_blank_lines_in_jsonl(s3_bucket: None):
    lines = '{"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "a"}\n\n{"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T01:00:00+00:00", "message": "b"}\n'
    compressed = gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))
    boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1").put_object(
        Bucket=TEST_BUCKET, Key=f"{TEST_PREFIX}/req-1.jsonl.gz", Body=compressed
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["a", "b"]

The issues are:
1. `boto3.client()` returning unknown type — needs `cast` + ignore
2. `s3_bucket: None` fixture parameter `reportUnusedParameter` — use `_s3_bucket` or add ignore

For the `s3_bucket` fixture — it's used implicitly (pytest injects it to set up the bucket). The conventional way to mark a pytest fixture as used-by-side-effect is to use `_` prefix. But the test function names are clear. Let me just rename to `_s3_bucket` in the test functions or add a `# noqa` to suppress:

Actually, looking at the test functions, the `s3_bucket` fixture param must be named `s3_bucket` to match the fixture definition. Using `_s3_bucket` would break fixture injection.

The fix is to add `# pyright: ignore[reportUnusedParameter]` on the test function parameter. But these are in the function signature, which black may split. The cleanest solution is to add `_ = s3_bucket` in the function body — but that would break the code structure.

Actually, in pytest, the fixture parameter is "used" by pytest. Pyright doesn't understand this. The best fix is to suppress with an ignore. Let me use a type annotation approach:

Actually the simplest fix is to use `_` as the variable name but pytest would need `_: None` which is valid — but pytest matches fixtures by parameter name, so `_s3_bucket` won't work.

The cleanest way: add `# pyright: ignore[reportUnusedParameter]` on each test function's parameter line. But with black, those also get split. Let me check how many characters the parameter line is:

`def test_get_logs_returns_entries_in_file_order(s3_bucket: None):` — 64 chars, well under 88. No splitting needed.

Let me add the ignores directly:

content.text:
The existing tests use `dynamodb_table: Table` — it's typed with a real boto3 type, so pyright knows it's "used" (it's a `Table` value). But `s3_bucket: None` — the fixture yields `None`, and pyright sees a `None`-typed variable that's never read.

The fix is to check how `test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` was passing. The fixture there yields `Table` which has a useful type. My `s3_bucket` fixture yields `None` — that's what causes the unused parameter warning.

The simple fix: change the fixture to yield a useful type, or just mark the parameter with `_` prefix convention... but pytest requires the exact fixture name.

Another approach: use the fixture result directly in the test, or suppress with a `# noqa` in a different way. Actually, the cleanest fix is to just change the fixture to return the S3 client, and use it in build functions. But that's a bigger refactor.

The simplest non-invasive fix: add `del s3_bucket` inside the test body. But that's ugly.

Actually, the best approach: use `pytest.mark.usefixtures` decorator instead of parameter injection:

import gzip

from moto import mock_aws

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import S3AuditLogReader

def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:

    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


@pytest.fixture
def s3_bucket() -> Generator[None, None, None]:

        yield

    ts1 = "2026-05-07T10:00:00+00:00"

import gzip
import json
from collections.abc import Generator
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Literal, cast

import boto3
import pytest
from moto import mock_aws

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import S3AuditLogReader

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    S3Client = Any  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]

TEST_BUCKET: Literal["test-audit-bucket"] = "test-audit-bucket"
TEST_PREFIX: Literal["audit-logs"] = "audit-logs"


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


def _s3_client() -> "S3Client":
    return cast("S3Client", boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1"))  # pyright: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]


@pytest.fixture
def s3_bucket() -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    with mock_aws():
        s3 = _s3_client()
        s3.create_bucket(
            Bucket=TEST_BUCKET,
            CreateBucketConfiguration={"LocationConstraint": "eu-west-1"},
        )
        yield


def build_reader() -> S3AuditLogReader:
    return S3AuditLogReader(bucket=TEST_BUCKET, prefix=TEST_PREFIX, s3_client=_s3_client())


def upload(id: str, entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> None:
    key = f"{TEST_PREFIX}/{id}.jsonl.gz"

content.text:
Black passes but pyright still warns. The `# type: ignore[misc]` only suppresses mypy errors, not basedpyright warnings. I need `# pyright: ignore`. Let me count the actual line length without the comment:

`    raw = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")` — 51 chars. Adding `  # pyright: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]` makes it 94 chars — 6 over. But what about `# pyright: ignore[reportUnknownVariableType]`? That's also long.

The key insight: the warning is `reportUnknownVariableType` for `raw` AND `reportUnknownMemberType` for `boto3.client`. Using the shorter `# pyright: ignore[reportUnknown]` won't work since it's not a valid rule name.

Let me use `# noqa: PGH003` (a ruff rule for blank `# type: ignore`) or just accept that for test files, we can use a different approach — suppress via `basedpyrightconfig.json`:

Actually, the real fix: look at how this was handled in `audit_logging.py` — the `cast(...)` pattern puts `boto3.client()` on its own line inside `cast()`, and when black reformats it, the `# pyright: ignore` stays on the `boto3.client(...)` line. But that only works if the inner line with the comment is under 88 chars.

Let me check how many chars `        "S3Client", boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")  # pyright: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]` is:
- `        ` = 8 spaces
- `"S3Client", boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")` = 55 chars
- `  # pyright: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]` = 44 chars
- Total: 8 + 55 + 44 = 107 chars — too long

What about using the `cast` without the separate variable:

Actually, the issue is only with `test_s3_audit_log_reader.py`. The same `boto3.client("dynamodb")` pattern in `audit_logging.py` works because black splits it as:

```python
    dynamodb = cast(
        "DynamoDBClient",
        boto3.client("dynamodb"),  # pyright: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]
    )
```

The key: `"dynamodb"` is shorter than `"s3", region_name="eu-west-1"`. Let me check:
- `        boto3.client("dynamodb"),  # pyright: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]` = 8 + 30 + 44 = 82 chars ✓
- `        boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1"),  # pyright: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]` = 8 + 47 + 44 = 99 chars ✗

I need to either use a shorter ignore comment or split the `s3` region_name out. Let me try `# pyright: ignore[reportUnknownVariableType]` — only 44 chars too. Actually wait, let me use only the necessary rules. The warning says `reportUnknownMemberType` for `boto3.client` (a partially unknown member). Let me check the actual rule name used in the warning:

The warning says "Type of `client` is partially unknown" — this is `reportUnknownMemberType`. But that's 44 chars for the ignore. I could use a shorter approach:

Let me try `  # pyright: ignore` (without specifying rule) — that suppresses ALL pyright warnings on the line:

input.prompt:

5. Lines 345, 350, 351: `reportAny` - MagicMock attributes typed as Any
6. Lines 367-368: `reportAny` - `s3` is Any, argument type is Any
7. Lines 375, 389: `reportAny` - `assert_called_once_with` is Any

9. Lines 384-397: same pattern as 367-375

The file is at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/tests/test_audit_logging.py`.

Key constraints:
- Black is also run (line length 88). Comments like `# pyright: ignore[...]` must be on the SAME LINE as the actual value being ignored, not on a closing paren.
- Do NOT use `Any` directly in type annotations without `# pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]` on the same line.
- The `_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]` TypeAlias pattern is already used in the file.
- For `MagicMock` attribute access warnings (`reportAny`), add `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` on the relevant lines.
- For `reportUnannotatedClassAttribute`: add type annotations to `_pages` and `_query_pages`.
- For `reportPrivateUsage`: add `# pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]` on those lines.
- For `reportUnusedCallResult`: assign result to `_`.
- For the `_make_stub_s3` return type `Any`: the function is already annotated as `-> Any: # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]` on line 337. The warning says "Return type is Any (reportAny)" — this should be suppressed with `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` on the same line as the return annotation.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
from collections.abc import Generator
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Literal

import boto3
import pytest
from boto3.dynamodb.conditions import Key
from moto import mock_aws
from types_boto3_dynamodb.service_resource import Table

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import AuditEntry, DynamoDBAuditLogger

TEST_TABLE_NAME: Literal["test_audit_table"] = "test_audit_table"


@pytest.fixture
def dynamodb_table() -> Generator[Table, None, None]:
    with mock_aws():
        dynamodb = boto3.resource("dynamodb", region_name="us-east-1")
        table = dynamodb.create_table(
            TableName=TEST_TABLE_NAME,
            KeySchema=[
                {"AttributeName": AuditEntry.ID_FIELD, "KeyType": "HASH"},
                {
                    "AttributeName": AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD,
                    "KeyType": "RANGE",
                },
            ],
            AttributeDefinitions=[
                {
                    "AttributeName": AuditEntry.ID_FIELD,
                    "AttributeType": "S",
                },
                {
                    "AttributeName": AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD,
                    "AttributeType": "N",
                },
            ],
            BillingMode="PAY_PER_REQUEST",
        )
        table.wait_until_exists()
        yield table


def build_audit_logger() -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    client = boto3.client("dynamodb", region_name="us-east-1")
    return DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name=TEST_TABLE_NAME,
        dynamodb=client,
    )


def test_log_persists_item_with_explicit_timestamp(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    result = dynamodb_table.get_item(
        Key={
            AuditEntry.ID_FIELD: "req-123",
            AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: int(timestamp.timestamp() * 1000),
        }
    )

    assert "Item" in result
    item = result["Item"]
    assert item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD] == "req-123"
    assert str(item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]) == "1778164245123"
    assert item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD] == "audit message"


def test_log_persists_item_with_generated_utc_timestamp(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-456", "generated timestamp message")

    after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    result = dynamodb_table.scan()

    assert "Items" in result
    items = [item for item in result["Items"] if item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD] == "req-456"]
    assert len(items) == 1
    item = items[0]
    assert item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD] == "generated timestamp message"
    timestamp_ms = int(str(item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]))
    assert timestamp_ms >= int(before.timestamp() * 1000)
    assert timestamp_ms <= int(after.timestamp() * 1000)


def test_log_relabels_non_utc_timezone_before_persisting(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=original_tz)
    expected_timestamp_ms = int(
        timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc).timestamp() * 1000
    )

    logger.log("req-789", "timezone message", timestamp)

    result = dynamodb_table.get_item(
        Key={
            AuditEntry.ID_FIELD: "req-789",
            AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: expected_timestamp_ms,
        }
    )

    assert "Item" in result
    item = result["Item"]
    assert str(item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]) == str(expected_timestamp_ms)
    assert item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD] == "timezone message"


def test_log_allows_multiple_entries_for_same_id(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    first_timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    second_timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 31, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-repeat", "first message", first_timestamp)
    logger.log("req-repeat", "second message", second_timestamp)

    result = dynamodb_table.query(
        KeyConditionExpression=Key(AuditEntry.ID_FIELD).eq("req-repeat")
    )

    assert "Items" in result
    items = result["Items"]
    assert len(items) == 2
    assert [item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD] for item in items] == [
        "first message",
        "second message",
    ]


def test_get_logs_returns_chronological_entries(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts3 = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-chrono", "first", ts1)
    logger.log("req-chrono", "third", ts3)
    logger.log("req-chrono", "second", ts2)

    results = logger.get_logs("req-chrono")

    assert len(results) == 3
    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["first", "second", "third"]
    assert results[0].timestamp == ts1
    assert results[1].timestamp == ts2
    assert results[2].timestamp == ts3


def test_get_logs_returns_only_entries_for_requested_id(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    ts = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-a", "entry for a", ts)
    logger.log("req-b", "entry for b", ts)

    results = logger.get_logs("req-a")

    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0].id == "req-a"
    assert results[0].message == "entry for a"


def test_get_logs_returns_empty_for_unknown_id(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()

    assert logger.get_logs("no-such-id") == []

content.text:
Here's a summary of the changes made to satisfy the pre-commit hook (`prek`) dependencies, primarily driven by `basedpyright` in strict mode:

---

## Core feature changes (`audit_logging.py`, `reporter_lambda.py`, `template.yaml`, etc.)

These were the actual feature — `get_logs` on all `AuditLogger` implementations, `S3AuditLogReader`, `FallbackAuditLogReader`, `ReporterFunction` Lambda, SAM/Makefile wiring.

---

## Type annotation fixes required by `basedpyright`

**Black vs pyright alignment** — the main recurring problem. Black reformats long lines by splitting them, moving `# pyright: ignore[...]` comments to a closing `)` line rather than the line where the actual offending expression sits. The solution was to:
- Use `TypeAlias` shorthands (`_DdbPage`, `_Body`) to keep annotated lines short enough that black doesn't split them
- Use two-statement patterns (`raw = boto3.client(...); return cast(...)`) so the ignore comment sits on the `boto3.client(...)` line, not a closing paren

**`boto3.client()` / `boto3.resource()` unknown types** — every call to these produces `reportUnknownVariableType` + `reportUnknownMemberType`. Fixed by replacing direct type annotations with `cast("TypeName", boto3.client(...))` and adding `# pyright: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]` on the `boto3.client()` line.

**`dict[str, Any]` in function signatures** — `reportExplicitAny` fires on each use of `Any`. Fixed by suppressing with `# pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]` on the parameter line itself (not the return type line), using the `_DdbPage` alias to avoid splitting.

**AWS Lambda Powertools decorators losing return type** — `@tracer.capture_lambda_handler` returns `Any`, so `lambda_handler`'s return type was inferred as `Any` in tests. Fixed by introducing an `invoke()` helper in tests that `cast`s the result to `dict[str, object]`.

**`MagicMock` attribute access** — `.get_logs.side_effect`, `.assert_called_once_with()` etc. are all `Any` on `MagicMock`. Suppressed with `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` on each access line.

**Unused fixture parameters** — `reportUnusedParameter` for `dynamodb_table: Table` and `s3_bucket: None` parameters that are only injected for side effects. Fixed by switching to `@pytest.mark.usefixtures("fixture_name")` decorator and removing the parameter from the function signature.

**`reportUnannotatedClassAttribute`** — stub classes needed class-level type annotations for attributes assigned only in methods (e.g. `_pages: list[_DdbPage]`).

**`reportUnusedCallResult`** — `s3.create_bucket(...)`, `s3.put_object(...)`, `reader.get_logs(...)` return values not assigned. Fixed with `_ = ...`.
